programs.title: Harmony Keepers: Guardians of golden snub-nosed monkeys
programs.desc: This documentary series pays tribute to the guardians of China’s rare wildlife and their habitats. Through the lens of ecological protection and preservation, the series features four iconic protected species in China: the Tibetan antelope, milu deer, black-necked cranes, and golden snub-nosed monkeys.

programs.title: Foodwise：Eating Global in Beijing
programs.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in search of global cuisines that can be found in Beijing, the 3000-year old capital of China. From honest farm-to-table fare, to the culinary heights of French haute cuisine, and even a cooking class and wine and dine event at a traditional courtyard home in the city center, we'll discover that in Beijing, you can taste the world.

programs.title: Foodwise: Quanzhou
programs.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May are in Quanzhou, the ancient starting point of the Maritime Silk Road. From its covered arcades and Roman columns to its Southeast Asian vibe, our hosts discover that Quanzhou truly is a melting pot of world cultures. Then there’s the food: From the local noodle porridge that utilizes Silk Road spices, to fried vinegar pork and a dessert made from a special algae, there’s plenty of unique dishes to try here. Over a cup of oolong, our hosts will discover how Quanzhou’s tea changed the world, and how trade with Arab civilizations influenced a local fishing village. So join us in Quanzhou, a city that UNESCO designated as an “Emporium of the World”.

programs.title: Foodwise: Xi'an
programs.desc: In this episode, Tianran He and Eva May venture to Xi'an, an ancient city with over 3000 years of history, capital to 13 Chinese dynasties. But first, our hosts are going to carb load on street food, from glutinous rice cakes and cakes made out of persimmons, to mutton soup with flatbread and the famous biangbiang noodles. They'll also taste the rich history of Xi'an by experiencing a Qin Dynasty-inspired banquet, and try millennia-old dishes such as trout grilled on a ceramic tile and calabash chicken, a Tang Dynasty delicacy.\nFrom the Terracotta Army to the Daming Palace, residence of the Tang emperors, to a dazzling nighttime boulevard that recreates the grandeur of the Tang Dynasty, there's so much to explore in Xi'an. So, get your camera and your stomach ready for CGTN's Foodwise in Xi'an.

programs.title: Breathing new life into century-old Yueju Opera
programs.desc: Yueju Opera is one of the five major Chinese operas. It is skillful in narration and sentiment and is popular across China. This timeless form of intangible cultural heritage now has new apprentices. We followed a young duo of Yueju Opera performers: eleven-year-old Wang Yuhan and her stage partner, nine-year-old Huang Xinru in Ningbo, Zhejiang Province, as they shared kindred spirits in inheriting the performing art of Yueju Opera.

programs.title: Wudang Martial Arts and Taichi
programs.desc: Join host Yegor on a journey into the heart of China’s Wudang Mountains—the spiritual cradle of Taoism and the birthplace of Wudang martial arts. Through in-depth conversations with renowned masters such as Zhong Xueyong, Cai Chongguang, Jake Pinnick, and Liu Xiang, this episode explores the philosophy, techniques, and living spirit of Wudang Quan and Taichi.\nFrom the internal cultivation of qi to the harmony between body and mind, Wudang martial arts are more than just physical training—they are a way of life rooted in the Tao. Alongside martial techniques, the program also delves into traditional Chinese music, calligraphy, and medicine, revealing how these disciplines come together in the pursuit of balance, self-awareness, and longevity.\nThis is not just a story of punches and postures, but of tradition, transformation, and the timeless search for harmony between humans and the universe.
